channel.title: Ethernet on EasyFPGA
channel.description: Recent content in Ethernet on EasyFPGA

item.title: Ethernet II + IPv4 + UDP Frame Structure Reference
item.description: A complete byte-level breakdown of an Ethernet II / IPv4 / UDP frame — the essential reference for FPGA-based network packet processing.

item.title: FPGA Implementation of RoCEv2
item.description:
What is the RoCE v2?

RoCEv2, or RDMA over Converged Ethernet version2 is a network protocol that enable Remote Direct Memory Access (RDMA) over standard Ethernet networks using UDP/IP. It’s designed to deliver high-throughput, low-latency communication while minimizing CPU usage-making it ideal for data centers, high-performance computing, and storage systems.

The link below is a lecture on RDMA. It provides a lot of useful information. https://www.coursera.org/learn/the-fundamentals-of-rdma-programming/home/info

RoCEv2 Packet Format

  • Ethernet Header

Standard Ethernet frame header.

item.title: Gigabit Ethernet with Xilinx FPGA and TEMAC IP
item.description: provides an overview of implementing Gigabit Ethernet using Xilinx FPGAs and the TEMAC IP core.
